qualification_packs.name: Four Wheeler Service Assistant
qualification_packs.job_description: A Four Wheeler Service Assistant is responsible for assisting the four wheeler service technician in repair, routine servicing and maintenance (including electrical and mechanical aggregates) of vehicles

nos_units.title: Organize work and resources (Service)
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about implementing safety, planning work, adopting sustainable practices for optimising use of resources
scope: Maintain safe and secure working environment
Perform work as per quality standards
Health and hygiene
Material/energy conservation practices
Effective waste management practices

performance_criteria.task: organise work as per organisation’s current health, safety and security policies and procedures
report any identified breaches in health, safety, and security policies and procedures to the designated person
identify the risks and hazards associated with work activities, their causes and prevention Perform work as per quality standards To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure work area is clean and tidy
ensure that work is accomplished as per the requirements within the specified timeline
ensure team goals are given preference over individual goals Health and hygiene To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
sanitize workstation and equipment regularly
clean hands with soap, alcohol-based sanitizer regularly
avoid contact with ill people and self-isolate in a similar situation
wear and dispose PPEs regularly and appropriately
report advanced hygiene and sanitation issues to appropriate authority
follow stress and anxiety management techniques Material/energy conservation practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
identify ways to optimise usage of material in various tasks/activities/processes
use resources, including water, in a responsible manner
check for spills/leakages in various tasks/activities/processes
plug spills/leakages and escalate to appropriate authority if unable to rectify
carry out routine cleaning of tools, machines and equipment
check if the equipment/machine is functioning normally before commencing work and rectify wherever required
report malfunctioning (fumes/sparks/emission/vibration/noise) and lapse in maintenance of equipment
ensure electrical equipment and appliances are properly connected and turned off when not in use Effective waste management practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
identify recyclable and non-recyclable, and hazardous waste generated
segregate waste into different categories
dispose non-recyclable waste appropriately
deposit recyclable and reusable material at identified location
follow processes specified for disposal of hazardous waste

knowledge.description: organisation procedures for health, safety and security, and individual role and responsibilities in this context
the organisations emergency procedures for different emergency situations and the importance of following the same
evacuation procedures for workers and visitors
how and when to report hazards as well as the limits of responsibility for dealing with hazards
potential hazards, risks and threats based on the nature of work
the implications of own work on the schedule and work of others
efficient utilisation of material and water
basics of electricity and prevalent energy efficient devices
ways to recognise common electrical problems
common practices of conserving electricity
common sources of pollution and ways to minimize it
categorisation of waste into dry, wet, recyclable, non-recyclable and items of single-use plastics
usage of different colours of dustbins
waste management and methods of waste disposal
significance of greening
organisation's policies to maintain personal health and hygiene at workplace

skills.description: read instructions/guidelines/standard operating procedures
complete statutory documents relevant to safety and hygiene
modify work practices to improve them
ask for clarifications from superior about the job requirement
work with supervisors/team members to carry out work related tasks
complete tasks efficiently and accurately within stipulated time
inform/report to concerned person in case of any problem
make timely decisions for efficient utilization of resources
write in at least one language and complete written work with attention to detail
record data on waste disposal at workplace
be punctual, utilize time and manage workload efficiently
evaluate strategies to maintain, enhance or reduce the intensity of heightened emotional response

nos_units.title: Interact effectively with colleagues, customers and others
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about communicating with customers and colleagues/superiors, either in own work group or in other work groups within organisation.
scope: Communicate effectively with colleagues, customers and others
Interact with supervisor or superior

performance_criteria.task: maintain clear communication with colleagues, customers and others, wherever needed, through all means i.e. face-to-face, telephonic or written
adjust communication styles to reflect gender and persons with disability (PwD) sensitivity
work in a way that shows respect for colleagues and others
follow the organisation’s policies and procedures while working in a team
respect personal space of colleagues and customers Interact with supervisor or superior To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
identify work requirements by receiving instructions from reporting supervisor
escalate problems to supervisors that cannot be handled including repairs and maintenance of machine
report the completed work
rectify errors as per feedback

knowledge.description: the importance of effective communication and establishing good working relationships with colleagues and supervisor
different methods of communication as per the circumstances
gender based concepts, issues and legislation

skills.description: read instructions/guidelines/procedures
listen effectively and orally communicate information
ask for clarification and advice from the concerned person
maintain positive and effective relationships with colleagues and customers
evaluate the possible solution(s) to the problem
deliver consistent and reliable service to customers
complete written work with attention to detail
check that the work meets customer requirements

nos_units.title: Assist in service, maintenance and repair of the vehicle
nos_units.description: An Assistant is responsible for assisting the four wheeler service technician in repair, routine servicing and maintenance (including electrical and mechanical aggregates) of 4 wheeler vehicles
scope: Assist in preparation for service, maintenance and repair of the vehicle
Provide assistance for service, maintenance and repair/replacement of vehicle parts or aggregates
Perform pollution check

performance_criteria.task: assist in positioning the vehicle on a suitable platform for service, maintenance and repair
assist the seniors by running errands such as collecting/fetching the required special service tools, measuring instruments, vehicle parts, gauges, fixtures, workshop supplies, etc.
report the malfunctioning, if any, in tools/equipment to the concerned person for rectification
report the malfunctions/repairs beyond own scope to the concerned person for rectification

prepare the work area by cleaning and placement of tools/equipment in an organised manner Provide assistance for service, maintenance and repair/replacement of vehicle parts or aggregates To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
identify the auto component/aggregate and their specifications
assist senior in visual inspection of the vehicle for any external impact/bend/leak/incorrect level/wear & tear
work carefully to ensure no damage is caused to any aggregate/component
comply with the Standard Operating Procedures and vehicle service manuals for repairing, servicing and using workshop tools and equipment
maintain documentation of each process as specified by OEM
assist senior in performing routine service/maintenance (inspect/correct/adjust/clean/lubricate) in vehicle/aggregate

perform routine service/maintenance (change/replenish/top-up - lubricants, fluids, coolant,filters etc.) and minor repair/replacement (wheels, wiper, mud flap, brake pad, brake shoes, wheel balancing etc.) in vehicle/aggregate under supervision of senior
assist in dismantling aggregate like suspension steering system, cooling system, engine gearbox etc. Perform pollution check To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
perform PUC (Pollution Under Control) check as per standard procedure using appropriate software and hardware
check the reading to confirm if these are within acceptable range and inform the concerned person about discrepancies, if any
check the previous PUC certificate for existing record or take data from registration certificate and enter details in software
prepare the PUC certificate

knowledge.description: Complete layout of the workshop
use of measuring equipment such as feeler gauges, torque wrench, multimeter, engineering rule (scale), battery charger, tester, hydrometer, tachometer etc.

use of routine service tools including fuel pressure testers, manifold gauge sets, oil pressure gauges, tire pressure gauges etc., and ball joint separators, bearing pullers, gear puller tools, slide hammers etc.
safety requirements for components/aggregates and equipment as prescribed by the OEM
safety, health and environmental policies and regulations at workplace and of automotive trade in general (e.g. safe practices while working in pits/under vehicles)

basic functioning of different types of engines such as diesel, petrol, electrical, gas, hybrid etc, drive line or drive train-clutch, gearbox (transmission) or transaxle (manual), propeller shaft, differential, drive axles, wheels, wheel rims and tyres, functioning of systems such as air supply, fuel, cooling, emission and exhaust, ignition, lubrication, steering, suspension, brake
identification of workshop tools, equipment, materials and their storage location
organisational and professional code of ethics and standards of practice
documentation requirements for each procedure
standard operating procedures recommended by the OEM for using tools and equipment to be followed as per the manufacturer instructions
workplace policies and schedules for housekeeping activities and equipment maintenance

functioning of batteries and power storage system, power-generating systems (including charging systems especially for electrical and hybrid vehicles), electrical wire harness, starting, lighting, horn, wiper and air-conditioning systems etc., energy recuperation systems, electronic systems including active and passive safety, media and other systems
Standard Operating Procedures of the organisation for inspection, servicing and repair of vehicles as per manufacturer's specification
the type, quality and codification system of components specified by the OEM for use as replacement parts
manufacturer specifications of components/aggregates and different sections of the vehicle such as dashboard, seat pockets, etc.
the instructions related to the grade and use of oils/lubricants, grease and other fluids specified by the OEM
standard approved procedure and parameters for performing pollution check
how to interpret the readings with respect to the approved ranges
functioning of the software for preparation of the PUC certificate

skills.description: communicate effectively at the work place
plan and organize work according to the principles of 5S

read the service circulars/sign boards placed in the workshop with respect to the overall process to be followed for service, repair and maintenance of the vehicle and safety related guidelines applicable for CNG/LPG/Electric vehicle
understand the need of the customer
analyse, evaluate and apply the information gathered from observation, experience, reasoning or communication to act efficiently
write in English/regional language

qualification_packs.name: Automotive Body Repair Technician
qualification_packs.job_description: An Automotive Body Repair Technician is responsible for carrying out repairs on damaged body and frame components of a vehicle following a collision or corrosion.

nos_units.title: Carry out repairs and replacement on non-structural body
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about the Automotive Body Repair Technician in assessing the damage to the vehicle body and then repairing or replacing the identified non structural body panels including plastic and glass components.
scope: Assist in damage assessment and repair estimate preparation
Prepare to perform repair/replace on non-structural panels or components
Perform repair/replace on non-structural panels or components
Perform post repair activities

performance_criteria.task: review the job card and obtain required information to determine the repair requirements
determine direction of impact, extent of damage and whether all the damages are the result of the same collision

assist supervisor or service advisor in visual inspection of the entire vehicle to identify and collect evidence of direct or indirect damages on body panels/assemblies/subassemblies/component that is found bent, broken, crushed, leak, rusted or missing
report the malfunctions/repairs in the vehicle beyond own scope to the concerned person

assist supervisor or service advisor in initial repair estimate preparation, by listing required consumables, fasteners, parts to be replace or repair (denting/cutting/welding) using manufacturer's part catalogue and vehicle repair manuals Prepare to perform repair/replace on non-structural panels or components To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
place the vehicle on suitable platform according to nature of job to be performed

collect appropriate workshop tools/equipment (hand tools, power tools, measuring devices, workshop equipment etc.) new panel, spare parts, consumables required for repair/replacement job on non-structural components and check their condition/calibration
report the malfunctions if any, in the tools/equipment/new parts to the person concerned for rectification

prepare self by wearing appropriate PPE and the work area by cleaning, placing tools/equipment in an organised manner Perform repair/replace on non-structural body panels or components To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
take precautions to avoid damage to the vehicle and its components while working on various vehicle body panels
use workshop tools/equipment/consumable (hand tools, power tools, measuring devices, sealer gun, suction cups etc.) required for the job as per OEM Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

use suitable method/technique to remove detachable parts such as upholstery, trims, accessories, windscreen, window glass, electrical window/seat operating mechanism, etc. and, tag removed items for repair, reuse and replacement as per manufacturers' guidelines and report additional repair requirement if noticed any hidden damages
place removed parts securely as per manufacturers' guidelines

use OEM or product supplier repair method excluding welding such as riveting, bonding, screwing, bolting etc. to carry out required repairs on various body parts including plastic and glass components/panels and reinstate original vehicle dimensions
apply appropriate sealers, foams, anti-corrosion coatings and sound dampening pads where ever applicable
reinstall non-structural removed/repaired/new panels/parts (pre or post paint job completion as per requirement) and correct the alignment of the panels and components
inspect grooves, angles, or gap allowances, of various body panels/components post re- fitting/replacement using micrometer, caliper, feeler gauge, etc. as per manufacturer's specifications
carry out water leak test to ensure proper sealing or adhesion of replaced rubber seal/weather strip or glass work activity, as per OEM recommended guidelines
maintain the documentation related to body repair/replacement job done on the vehicle perform post repair activities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure completeness of tasks assigned before releasing the vehicle for the next procedure
dispose off materials such as empty boxes, empty cans/tube etc, scrap of damage parts/panels as per organization’s policies
return leftover consumable/parts, tools/equipment to the person concerned and report if any malfunction observed
inspect repaired vehicles for proper functioning and test drive vehicles to ensure proper alignment and handling

knowledge.description: about the Automotive Industry in India, workshop structure and role and responsibilities of different people in the workshop
SOPs regarding receiving vehicles, opening job card, allocation of work, invoicing, vehicle delivery, handling complaints etc.

identification, nomenclature, manufacturer's specifications of various consumable/material (seam sealers, abrasives, masking tape, adhesion promoter, cleaning agents, primer, applicator, sealants etc.) hand tools, manual and electric dent pullers, welding equipment etc. and vehicle parts and body panels,
about overall functioning of various types of collision repair equipment and material
about workshop tools, equipment, materials, their usage, storage and maintenance
various tools used to carry out remove and replace operations and their safe/correct usage
range of methods for removing and replacing various exterior and interior body panel or parts such as trims, lining, bumpers, windscreens, window glass, seats, window regulator, sunroof etc.
various methods used to align replaced parts and panels to reinstate manufacturers original settings
safety requirements while working on the vehicle for collision repair work including glass and aluminum repairs
characteristics of various commonly used glass parts in the vehicle such as Tempered/Toughened glass & Laminated glass
characteristics of various commonly used plastic parts in the vehicle such as Polypropylene (PP), Poly Carbonate (PC), Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), etc.
various personal protective equipment required for collision repair job and their usage
type of vehicle body and chassis damage/defects their cause, prevention and rectification
features of body construction relating to strength and collision protection
features and purpose of structural and non-structural panels
characteristics of common metals such as mild steel, aluminum, aluminum alloy, high strength steels (HSS), ultra-high strength steels (UHSS)
direction and weight of damage force as well as the impact at the point of collision
processes and procedures for preparing replacement panel work and panel fixing positions
the importance of realigning structural parts and assemblies to reinstate vehicle integrity and driving characteristics
reinstating suitable corrosion protection to replaced parts
Manufacturers removal, replacement, repair, and testing procedures related to vehicle body and frame
documentation required on the job (including job cards, work sheets, etc. regarding the basic details of repair and service performed
organisational and professional code of ethics and standards of practice
safety, health and environmental policies and regulations for the work place as well as for automotive trade in general

skills.description: read and interpret workplace related documentation
communicate using terms, names, grades and other nomenclature pertaining to the automotive trade
analyse and apply the information gathered from observation, experience, reasoning or communication to act efficiently
identify potential workplace problem and take suitable action
write in English/regional language

nos_units.title: Carry out repair/replacement by cutting and welding on
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about the Automotive Body Repair Technician repairing or replacing by cutting and welding on various sheet metal (structural and non-structural) including aluminum and assisting senior to carry out vehicle body and frame measurements
scope: Prepare to perform cutting and welding on vehicle body panels
Perform cutting and welding on vehicle body panels
Perform post repair activities

performance_criteria.task: review the job card and obtain required information to determine the repair requirements
determine direction of impact, extent of damage and whether all the damages are the result of the same collision
place the vehicle on suitable platform according to nature of job to be performed

collect appropriate workshop tools/equipment (hand tools, power tools, measuring devices, cutting and welding equipment- for steel and aluminum, etc.) new body panel, spare parts, consumables required for collision repair and check their condition/calibration
report the malfunctions/repairs in the vehicle, tools/equipment/new parts which is beyond own scope to the concerned person
prepare self by wearing appropriate PPE and the work area by cleaning, placing tools/equipment in an organised manner
take precautions to avoid damage to the vehicle and its components Perform cutting and welding on vehicle body/frame To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:

apply suitable technique to use workshop tools/equipment (hand tools, power tools, measuring devices, cutting and welding equipment, etc.) required for the job as per OEM Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

use suitable method to remove detachable parts such as upholstery, trims, accessories, window glass, electrical window/seat operating mechanism, etc. to gain access to damaged body panels/body structure/reinforcement, tag removed items for replacement and report additional repair requirement if noticed any hidden damages
seek assistance from senior to carryout rough pulling for body alignment process
cut and remove damaged welded or structural panels (steel or aluminum) with minimal disturbance to surrounding panels and prepare surface of damaged area and new panel appropriately for installation

apply suitable technique while installing/replacing new panel in secured manner for correct alignment with the vehicle body (structural and non-structural panels such as running board, quarter panel, side members, pillar, etc.)
use various welding technique to weld new panels or panel assemblies(steel and aluminum) as per manufacturers’ recommended specifications
assist senior in the measurement of vehicle body dimension to ensure correct alignment or final correction
dress weld seams using sanding/grinding operations
use various techniques to apply body seam sealers and for rust prevention treatment as specified by OEM

reinstall removed or new parts such as upholstery, trims, accessories, window glass, electrical window/seat operating mechanism, etc. pre or post paint job completion as per requirement Perform post repair activities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
maintain the documentation related to body repair/replacement job done on the vehicle
ensure completeness of tasks assigned before releasing the vehicle for the next procedure
dispose off materials such as empty boxes, empty cans/tube etc, scrap of damage parts/panels as per organization’s policies
return leftover consumable/parts, tools/equipment to the person concerned and report if any malfunction observed
inspect repaired vehicles for proper functioning and test drive vehicles to ensure proper alignment and handling

SOPs regarding receiving vehicles, opening job card, allocation of work, invoicing, vehicle delivery, handling complaints etc.

various personal protective equipment required for collision repair job and their usage
type of vehicle body and chassis damage/defects their cause, prevention and rectification
features of body construction relating to strength and collision protection
features and purpose of structural and non-structural panels
characteristics of common metals such as mild steel, aluminum, aluminum alloy, high strength steels (HSS), ultra-high strength steels (UHSS)
direction and weight of damage force as well as the impact at the point of collision
principles of pulling systems including fixed post, swinging arm and vector systems
principles of operation and adjustment of welding systems used for panel replacement including MAGS, Resistance spot and MIG brazing

the importance of realigning structural parts and assemblies to reinstate vehicle integrity and driving characteristics
reinstating suitable corrosion protection to replaced parts
Manufacturers removal, replacement, repair, and testing procedures related to vehicle body and frame

qualification_packs.job_description: The individual is responsible to assist Automotive Paint Repair Technician by preparing vehicle body and other surfaces for painting, which includes masking, sanding, cleaning, putty and primer application.

nos_units.title: Assist in vehicle painting by preparing vehicle body and
nos_units.description: This NOS is about assisting Automotive Paint Repair Technician by preparing vehicle body and other surfaces for painting through masking, sanding, cleaning, putty and primer application.
scope: Prepare for pre-painting work of the vehicle/panel
Prepare the surface for painting
Post surface preparation activities

performance_criteria.task: review the job card and understand scope of work
assist in positioning the vehicle on a suitable platform and put vehicle protective cover (seat, steering and gear knob cover)
identify the various body panels in the vehicle
inspect body panels for extent of paint damage (using touch and feel method) or body work (such as denting, welding) done by Auto Body Repair Technician
collect appropriate workshop tools/equipment, new body panel, spare parts, consumables (putty, abrasives, masking paper, etc.) required for surface preparation and check their condition/calibration

prepare self by wearing appropriate PPE and the work area by cleaning, placing tools/equipment in an organised manner Prepare the surface for painting To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
take precautions to avoid damage to the vehicle and its components while working on various vehicle body panels
use various consumables (cloth, degreaser etc) and suitable techniques such as wipe-on, wipe-off, air blowing etc. to clean the surface as per OEM SOP, at different stages of surface preparation
cover the surrounding surface and adjacent body panels (not to be painted), with masking tape and paper to prevent scratches/overspray while sanding and applying primer surface respectively
use suitable abrasive and sanding tools to remove paint beyond the damaged area by 1 - 2 inch (ideally removing the paint to the bare metal)
mix body fillers/ putty as per recommended ratio, apply body fillers using spatula within feather edged area and cure as specified by OEM
follow SOP to sand the body filler with recommended abrasive and tools, use dry guide coat between sanding steps to highlight imperfections in repair area until final refinement
ensure the surface is free from contamination, mix primer as per recommended ratio, apply primer on the surface using spray gun and cure as specified by OEM
follow SOP to sand the primer with recommended abrasive and tools, use dry guide coat between sanding steps to highlight imperfections in repair area until final refinement
maintain the documentation related to surface preparation job done on the vehicle Post surface preparation activities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
inspect surface finish post repair and report to supervisor/service advisor in case of any discrepancy
ensure completeness of tasks assigned before releasing the vehicle for the next procedure
dispose off materials such as old abrasive paper, empty cans/tube etc, scrap of damage parts/panels as per organization’s policies
return leftover consumable/parts, tools/equipment to the person concerned and report if any malfunction observed

nos_units.title: Carry out service, repair and overhaul of vehicle engine and
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about carrying out diagnosis of fault, service, repairs and overhaul of the engine and allied mechanical aggregates (like turbocharger).
scope: Prepare for service, repair, overhaul of engine and allied aggregates
Perform service, repair and overhaul of engine and allied aggregates
Perform post service/repair activities

performance_criteria.task: review the job card and understand work to be carried out
identify the auto components related to the various aggregates in the vehicle
identify the precise location of the faults in the engine and allied systems of the vehicle
test ride the vehicle to assess the requirement of calibration, other adjustments and repairs if any, in the engine and allied systems
diagnose any other direct or indirect faults in vehicle's engine and allied systems as per OEM SOP
collect workshop tools/measuring devices/test equipment required for the job and check their condition/calibration
report the malfunctions if any, in the tools/equipment to the person concerned for rectification
inspect the components requiring replacement due to continuous wear and tear such as belts, timing chain, engine oil and filters

remove and test engine and allied system components wherever applicable as per OEM SOP Perform service, repair and overhaul of engine and allied aggregates To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
report the malfunctions/repairs in the vehicle beyond own scope to the concerned person
seek approval from relevant persons about repairs/overhauling which are economically or technically infeasible
take precautions to avoid damage to the vehicle and its components while working on various aggregates
use workshop tools/measuring devices/equipment required for the job as per OEM Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
remove parts relevant to engine and place them securely as specified by OEM
dismantle engine and allied aggregates, if required and report additional repair requirement
clean and condition dismantled components, including mechanical and electrical aggregates, prior to assembly
inspect the machining/repair done by outside source/local machining garages
perform repair/replacement/calibration/overhaul in engine/allied aggregates such as cylinder head, turbo charger, fuel pump, etc.
refill/replace, as required, the appropriate grade of coolants, engine oil, other lubricant/fluids in the engine aggregates as per OEM guidelines
maintain the documentation related to inspection, servicing and repair of the vehicle Perform post service/repair activities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
check the performance of vehicle/aggregate post repair and report to supervisor/service advisor if further inspection is required by another specialist
ensure completeness of tasks assigned before releasing the vehicle for the next procedure
dispose off materials such as old batteries, scrap of failed parts/aggregates as per organization’s policies
return leftover consumable/parts, tools/equipment to the person concerned and report if any malfunction observed
perform scheduled checks, calibration and timely repairs for workshop tools, equipment and workstations

nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about an Automotive Body Repair Assistant who is responsible for assisting repair and replacement of the vehicle body components and identifies any other repairs requirements in the vehicle as a result of damage to the body components.
scope: Prepare to perform minor damage repairs on vehicle body panels
Perform minor damage repair on vehicle body panels
Perform post vehicle body repair activities

performance_criteria.task: review the job card and understand scope of work
assist in positioning the vehicle on a suitable platform and put vehicle protective cover (seat, steering and gear knob cover)
identify the various body panels in the vehicle

use suitable abrasive and sanding tools to remove paint beyond the damaged area by 1 - 2 inch (ideally removing the paint to the bare metal)

use suitable tools, equipment and technique such as hammer and dolly, shrinking method, washer welding etc.to fix minor dents, dimples, bulge in body metal and file, grind, sand the surface to give smooth finish

apply various body seam sealers as specified by OEM, wherever applicable

assist in using computerized tools to evaluate the structural integrity and alignment of the vehicle
ensure completeness of tasks assigned before releasing the vehicle for the next procedure

Perform post service/repair activities

Prepare to perform diagnostic tests
Perform tests to identify the root cause of fault
Conclude the repair solution for the fault

performance_criteria.task: review the job card, obtain required information from customer/service advisor to assess service and repair requirements
identify the auto components related to the various aggregates in the vehicle
check the functioning of vehicle systems such as lighting system, air conditioning system etc.
conduct test drive to check vehicle performance and identify/validate the faults
conduct visual inspection of the vehicle to identify defects in HV (High Voltage) components
assess mechanical components/aggregates such as brake pad/shoe, wheel cylinder, mountings, bushes, control arms etc. of the vehicle for any external impact/bend/leak/incorrect level/wear & tear
determine the precise location of faults in vehicle systems Prepare to perform diagnostic tests To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure no HV activity is being conducted around workstation prior to commencement of work
prepare the vehicle according to nature of job to be performed: general and mechanical job on the vehicle/electrical work in non-live state/troubleshoot and replace parts in live state of HV system
mark the vehicle and safeguard the working area during electrical work
collect workshop tools/measuring device/equipment required for fault diagnosis in vehicle systems and check their condition/calibration

report the malfunctions if any, in the tools/equipment to the person concerned for rectification Perform tests to identify the root cause of fault To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
take precautions to avoid damage to the vehicle and its components during diagnosis or troubleshooting the faults

use checklists and OEM Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to understand if the fault is because of improper charging, loose/poor contacts of pins in wiring harness connectors and their connection, improper driving style etc.

select and apply the appropriate device/equipment to make inspection and diagnose deficiencies/faults in various systems such as Body management systems, Braking and stability control systems, Drive line systems, Battery management system, Telematics system, etc.
use manufacturer's and component supplier’s specifications to identify duplicate or defective component/parts which cannot be detected during visual inspection
follow SOP set out for troubleshooting and perform tests using various mechanical, electrical/electronic measuring devices/tester/diagnostic tools/software to identify/isolate a fault

perform diagnostic task on the HV system as per following stages: general and mechanical tasks on the vehicle which do not require isolation of the HV systems, electrical work in non- live state of the HV systems, troubleshoot and replace parts in live state of the HV system
diagnose indirect faults if any, in vehicle's mechanical, electrical and electronic systems as per OEM SOP
report the malfunctions in the vehicle where solution is not available or in case of new premature failures, to the concerned person along with preliminary diagnostic details
dismantle and reassemble aggregates of a vehicle for fault diagnosis Conclude the repair solution for the fault To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
maintain the documentation related to inspections and troubleshooting performed on the vehicle
interpret results as required
compare results of diagnostic inspections/tests with vehicle specifications and regulatory requirements
validate the options for repair or replacement
prepare final proposal regarding repair or replacement requirements with justification

Perform work as per quality standards

Effective waste management practices

ensure work area is clean and tidy

wear and dispose PPEs regularly and appropriately

follow stress and anxiety management techniques Material/energy conservation practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:

use resources, including water, in a responsible manner
check for spills/leakages in various tasks/activities/processes
plug spills/leakages and escalate to appropriate authority if unable to rectify
carry out routine cleaning of tools, machines and equipment
check if the equipment/machine is functioning normally before commencing work and rectify wherever required
report malfunctioning (fumes/sparks/emission/vibration/noise) and lapse in maintenance of equipment
ensure electrical equipment and appliances are properly connected and turned off when not in use Effective waste management practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:

dispose non-recyclable waste appropriately

nos_units.title: High voltage and fire safety practices
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about following high voltage and fire safety practices at the workplace.
scope: Hight voltage safety practices
Fire safety practices

performance_criteria.task: make sure to wear personal safety equipment and that it fits properly, is worn correctly and is not damaged in any way
consult the common and manufacturer specific instructions where available
examine the high voltage battery visually for physical, mechanical damage, intrusion and leakage
if the high voltage battery is identified as damaged handle, inform the relevant person in accordance with manufacturer specific instruction and applicable national legislation and guideline
ensure the area around the EV is restricted and marked, before removal of high voltage battery
use only the test instruments, and insulated tools rated for the voltage and current specified
don't wear any jewelry or other articles that could accidentally contact circuitry and conduct current, or get caught in moving parts
set up your work area away from possible grounds that you may accidentally contact
use a grounded safety chain or cable when working with high voltage
never touch, cut or open high voltage power cable or high voltage components without personal protective equipment

do not allow open flames near, apply heat to the EV battery or do not expose to high temperature, e.g. long period in direct sunlight Fire safety practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
before starting work, use a non-contact infrared thermometer to check if the battery is heating up. If so, move the vehicle to a safe isolation area and call the fire department.
don't overload circuits and check cords & extensions before starting work
use warning signage and cordon off the area. Lock the remote key to prevent the vehicle from moving or the electrical systems from accidentally operating
unplug the service plug to disconnect the battery from the high voltage (HV) lines. Wait 5–10 minutes after the supercapacitors have discharged before working on the vehicle
use appropriate fire extinguisher (ABC, BC) incase of any fire
evacuate to a safe place and do not let other people approach the site incase of fire accident
when a vehicle fire occurs due to the battery, there is a risk of a second fire. Contact your local fire emergency responders when towing the vehicle
report any electrical faults to appropriate person to avoid any accident

knowledge.description: various ways that can make accidents while working on high voltage area or working near high voltage equipment

nos_units.title: Perform maintenance of electric vehicle (EV)
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about carrying out operations during the preventive and breakdown maintenance of electric vehicle (EV) and documenting the operations carried out.
scope: Plan for the maintenance activities
Carrying out maintenance of the EV
Conducting trials and documentation

performance_criteria.task: identify the EV, its manufacturer's specifications and functioning from the user manual and vehicle drawings
interpret maintenance checklist and coordinate with the superior for confirming the maintenance tasks
read the maintenance schedule of EV and plan the time and schedule for conducting the maintenance
identify and arrange the tools, consumables and spare parts required during the task Carrying out maintenance of the EV To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
follow safety practices during maintenance activities as per organisational SOP

perform basic health check-up of vehicle for defined performance parameters such as battery status, sensor calibration, actuators status, other electronic circuitry response etc. as specified in the maintenance checklist
dismantle the vehicle components and replace/change the electrical and electronic system spare parts and consumables of the vehicle as per the schedule

check the internal conditions of vehicle parts i.e. bearings, shafts, battery systems, motors, wiring harness and connectors, Electronic Control Unit (ECU), automatic lock system and other similar parts etc. to test its working status and expected conditions

conduct breakdown maintenance and check the systems of the vehicle to find out root cause of the problems like any leakage, short circuit in parts, breakage of wires and clamps, unusual contact of electrical wires with other parts etc. in the vehicle and discuss the same with senior if required
use electronic meters like Multimeter, Digital Storage Oscilloscope (DSO) and other software tools to identify any bugs in the vehicle system
record the readings of important parameters e.g. battery, other high voltage sections etc. with help of specialists/experts, as necessary
take immediate/permanent corrective actions after discussing with senior and clean, change or repair the vehicle components as per requirement
use recommended consumables, tools and equipment for service and repair of the EV as per Standard Operating Procedures (SOP)
dispose off waste materials such as failed parts/aggregates, as per organisation’s policies Conducting trials and documentation To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
assemble back all the components of the vehicle as per the drawing and prepare it for conducting the trials
fasten the components/subassemblies together by using specified screws, nuts and bolts
conduct trials of the vehicle and verify that specified parameters are attained with no abnormalities
change the maintenance due/status sticker on the vehicle
give suggestions to seniors for appropriate action based on findings during the breakdown maintenance to ensure that such breakdowns are not repeated
record all repairs carried out, time taken and unplanned tasks encountered during the maintenance activities
ensure that all maintenance points are adequately considered and report the superiors (manufacturing and maintenance department) about maintenance activity done on the vehicle

knowledge.description: company's maintenance standards and processes
different types of EVs manufactured by the company
different components/aggregates as well as auto component manufacturer's specifications for the same

basic technology used in and functioning of various systems and components of the vehicle such as batteries, body management system, telematics, brake system, air-conditioning systems, active & passive safety system, media and other systems (including electrical machines and devices used in electric vehicles such as: generator, Direct Current (DC)/Electric Charge (AC) and DC/DC converters, AC motor, DC motor, charging systems etc.)
interconnection of systems with each other and effect of one system on other system

fundamental terms, laws and principles of electricity used in EV such as: principles of storing electrical voltage, ohms law, voltage, current (AC/DC/HV), resistance, power, capacitance, electrostatics, magnetic, inductance, discrete electronic components, radio frequency, automotive communication protocols such as CAN, LIN, etc.
symbols, units and terms used in wiring diagrams associated with electrical/electric systems/components of the vehicle
legal regulations that need to be taken into account for handling electric vehicles in the workshop
how to read maintenance schedules and checklists recommended by the manufacturer
how to read vehicle layout or drawing and wiring diagrams to understand its structure
Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) recommended by OEM for using tools and equipment required
how to collect and store consumables, spare parts, tools, etc. as per organisational procedures
use of appropriate PPE, material handling equipment and tools for completing the maintenance tasks
corrective actions for common faults and failures in EV
documentation required regarding repair, maintenance and service performed

skills.description: read and interpret drawings, work instructions, user manuals and process documents
communicate the maintenance activities requirements to the supervisor and co-workers
record operation and maintenance information related to equipment in English/regional language
recognise a workplace problem and take suitable action
analyse and apply the information gathered from observation, experience, reasoning or communication to act efficiently
plan and organise work as per the work requirements
complete the assigned tasks as per schedule

nos_units.title: Manage work and resources (Research and Development)
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about implementing safety, planning work, adopting sustainable practices for optimising the use of resources.
scope: Maintain safe and secure working environment
Maintain Health and Hygiene
Effective waste management practices
Material/energy conservation practices

performance_criteria.task: identify hazardous activities and the possible causes of risks or accidents in the workplace
implement safe working practices for dealing with hazards to ensure safety of self and others
conduct regular checks of the machines with support of the maintenance team to identify potential hazards
ensure that all the tools/equipment/fasteners/spare parts are arranged as per specifications/utility into proper trays, cabinets, lockers as mentioned in the 5S guidelines/work instructions
organise safety drills or training sessions to create awareness amongst others on the identified risks and safety practices
fill daily check sheet to report improvements done and risks identified
ensure that relevant safety boards/signs are placed on the shop floor for the safety of self and others

report any identified breaches in health, safety and security policies and procedures to the designated person Maintain Health and Hygiene To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure workplace, equipment, restrooms etc. are sanitized regularly
ensure team is aware about hygiene and sanitation regulations and following them on the shop floor
ensure availability of running water, hand wash and alcohol-based sanitizers at the workplace
report advanced hygiene and sanitation issues to appropriate authority
follow stress and anxiety management techniques and support employees to cope with stress, anxiety etc
wear and dispose PPEs regularly and appropriately Effective waste management practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure recyclable, non-recyclable and hazardous wastes are segregated as per SOP

ensure proper mechanism is followed while collecting and disposing of non-recyclable, recyclable and reusable waste Material/energy conservation practices To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
ensure malfunctioning (fumes/sparks/emission/vibration/noise) and lapse in maintenance of equipment are resolved effectively
prepare and analyze material and energy audit reports to decipher excessive consumption of material and water
identify possibilities of using renewable energy and environment friendly fuels
identify processes where material and energy/electricity utilization can be optimized

the organisation's emergency procedures for different emergency situations and the importance of following the same
evacuation procedures for workers and visitors

waste management techniques

nos_units.title: Manage integration of IIoT sensors, edge devices and
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about managing tasks related to integration of IIoT sensors, IO-Links, Machines, robots and automation systems using industrial networking protocols, IIOT devices used in manufacturing processes to meet the specification set by the organization.
scope: Installation of IIOT elements as per the industrial network architecture and protocols
Integration and establishment of communication using I/O link master and network protocols
Perform post-installation activities

performance_criteria.task: design/interpret the network consists of devices, automation system and robots

support the IIOT technicians during selection and installation of the suitable network protocols like MODBUS, CC-LINK, PROFINET, PROFIBUS, OPC UA, MQTT etc. based on the communication networking system required
analyse the installed sensors, IO-Link trans-receivers, machines, automation elements, system and robots into different layers of network architecture like field devices, control devices, network
verify the network that consists of devices, automation system and robots as per SOP

ensure that IIOT technicians are connecting the appropriate intelligent devices and system using suitable network topology (STAR, LINE, RING) as per network design document Integration and establishment of communication using I/O link master and network protocols To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
support the IIOT technicians during connecting the automation elements like sensors, machines, and field control devices to I/O link master as per SOP
ensure that cable installed between devices is in align with the signaling parameters like bend radius, signal ground, terminal resistor, cable length etc.
establish and check the communication between automation system, intelligent devices, and robots by doing parameter setting like baud rate, distance, station ID and station type
support during setting of the network parameters of automation system on the device manufacturers software as per SOP and organizational guidelines
start the automation devices, system in the network and look for healthy communication between them
ensure physical security of the network contains IIOT edge devices, IIOT sensors, machines, robots and automation system
ensure that network has appropriate protection from the unauthorized access or malicious internet activities
ensure only authorized devices should be able to connect to the network Perform post-installation activities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
conduct the trials of system as per the plan to align it with existing or new manufacturing process
handover the system to production team & train them on it as per organizational guidelines and procedures
prepare and maintain documents and records such as experience under development, TGW /TGR faced during process trials etc. As a reference for future development

nos_units.title: Designing and Selection of Technology layer to manage
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about selection & designing of technology layer to manage security threats across the communication networks between IIoT sensors, IO-Links, machines, robots, and automation systems.
scope: Designing of network security architecture
Installation of application layer as per network security design
Perform network monitoring & threat assessment

performance_criteria.task: read and interpret e-plan or project document received from the manager
select appropriate industrial software (networking window) as per the project requirements
select appropriate core and auxiliary support process as per the project document
select appropriate security parameters for data present in edge computing devices, cloud platforms, open-source databases
define the manufacturing entities based on criticality and security threat levels in network security architecture
ensure that only authorized devices/systems should be able to connect to the network

design the network security system to ensure sufficient security levels are in place from device manufacturer, standard device installation & software requirement Installation of application layer as per network security design To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
select and install the suitable network protocols like MODBUS, CC-LINK, PROFINET, PROFIBUS, OPC UA, MQTT etc. based on the communication networking system
set the security parameters of system on the networking window as per SOP and organizational guidelines
configure and troubleshoot network security hardware like switches, routers, firewalls, WLAN, and Virtual Private Networks
select the devices/system mapped into application layer based on their functions & sensitivity of data

ensure that solution architecture (application layer) of the software performs day-to-day security processes such as threat and vulnerability management Perform network monitoring & threat assessment To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
evaluate criticality and security of threat levels of manufacturing entities
analyze data security performance metrics to highlight the threats in comparison with network security parameters
maintain and update the communication status of physical systems in the manufacturing process
implement regular threat assessment across devices to strengthen resistance against attack

nos_units.title: Detect & Respond to security Incidents & restore the
nos_units.description: This NOS unit is about detecting & responding to security incidents & restoring the communication in affected manufacturing entities.
scope: Detect the security incidents
Classify, prioritize & respond to the security incidents based on threat levels
Restoration & recovery of communication on affected manufacturing entities

performance_criteria.task: monitor the communication status & behavior of edge & cloud computing devices present in the IIOT network by using monitoring applications
monitor the status of field and control device in the IIOT network
interpret the field & control device status with edge computing device data on the dashboard
check for third party network interference sources and take precautionary measures so that it should not affect the communications

ensure readiness of software protocol & threat assessment processes to reliable detect incidents Classify, prioritize & respond to the security incidents based on threat levels To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
record, classify and prioritize information on security incidents using standard templates and tools
generate report from the affected manufacturing entities on threat level and submit it to manager
review the reports and consult with the manager for taking appropriate actions
respond to threat assessments on edge computing devices using automated security assessment tools after getting permission from manger

execute post-incident processes and procedures in line with security policies, procedures, and guidelines Restoration & recovery of communication on affected manufacturing entities To be competent, the user/individual on the job must be able to:
implement restoration & recovery plan given by manager
ensure timely restoration of devices and systems affected by security incident
evaluate the impact of security incidents in the manufacturing entities
assign information security incidents promptly to appropriate people for investigation/ action
